1. Primary Request and Intent:

**Request 4 (final):** "what next?" — establish the next concrete action.

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **Project**: PHP→Python migration of TT-RSS (RSS aggregator). Source: `source-repos/ttrss-php/`. Target: `target-repos/ttrss-python/`. Stack: Flask, SQLAlchemy, PostgreSQL, Celery+Redis, pluggy, feedparser+lxml.
- **Semantic verification**: Ensuring Python code is behaviorally equivalent to PHP source, not just structurally similar.
- **40-category discrepancy taxonomy (D01-D40)**: Organized into 6 domains — SQL semantics (D01-D10), Type system (D11-D16), Data flow (D17-D22), Session/state (D23-D28), Return values (D29-D33), Features/behavior (D34-D40).
- **Semantic traps**: PHP→Python patterns that consistently cause discrepancies — `empty("0")` falsy difference, `intval` vs `int()`, `isset`, `??` null coalescing, `strtotime` permissiveness, `mb_substr`, `preg_match` return type, `$_REQUEST` merge order, `@` error suppression, etc.
- **Integration pipeline verification**: 8 pipelines (feed update 12-step, article search 8-step, API lifecycle 6-step, auth flow 5-step, counter cache 4-step, OPML roundtrip, digest generation, plugin lifecycle) with explicit cross-function boundary contracts.
- **Complexity-tiered triage**: 52 Tier 1 functions (deep line-by-line audit), ~150 Tier 2 (standard), ~270 Tier 3 (quick check), 37 ORM models (schema deep check).
- **Cross-workstream sweeps**: Systemic pattern checks across all code — `get_user_pref` callers (D11 type coercion), session reads (D23/D24), GUID construction (D18), cache invalidation order (D37).
- **MADR ADR format**: Architecture Decision Records with Status, Context, Options, Trade-off Analysis, Decision, Consequences.
- **Consistency rule (AGENTS.md Rule 16)**: Any status/decision change must update ALL referencing locations in the same commit.

---

---

5. Problem Solving:

**Core problem solved**: The v2 verification plan had good inventory (472 functions listed) but a shallow 18-code taxonomy that missed the majority of real discrepancy categories. Concrete sampling of 6 function pairs proved this — 60+ discrepancies found in 15+ categories that v2 didn't define. The solution was to derive the taxonomy empirically from the sampled discrepancies, then generalize to 40 codes, and add integration pipeline verification to catch cross-function bugs that per-function audits miss.

**Key insight documented**: Individual function verification catches ~60% of bugs; the other ~40% come from data flowing through multiple functions with incompatible assumptions (e.g., GUID built in `update_feed` must match the lookup format in `upsert_entry`; PHP per-article commits allow partial success, Python all-or-nothing means failures roll back all articles).

**Critical concrete discrepancies found by sampling** (these are now in the taxonomy as canonical examples):
- D17: `update_rss_feed` content priority INVERTED — PHP: `get_content() ?: get_description()` (full content first); Python: `entry.get("summary") or content[0]["value"]` (summary first). Every article with both fields shows different text.
- D18: GUID construction — PHP prepends `owner_uid` + applies SHA1; Python uses raw feedparser `entry["id"]`. Same article in two users' feeds has different GUIDs in PHP, identical in Python — breaks dedup.
- D11: `get_pref` systemic — PHP returns typed bool/int; Python returns raw string. Every pref consumer (`make_init_params`, `sanitize`, `queryFeedHeadlines`, etc.) affected.
- D35: `sanitize` hook — PHP passes 5 args to `hook_sanitize`; Python passes 4 (omits `article_id`). Plugins using `article_id` silently lose context.
- D23: `make_init_params` — 13 session/config keys absent (`bw_limit`, `csrf_token`, `cookie_lifetime`, `icons_url`, `php_platform`, `php_version`, `sanity_checksum`, `max_feed_id`, `num_feeds`, `widescreen`, `simple_update`, `theme`, `plugins`).
- D14: `catchup_feed` boundary — PHP `$feed < LABEL_BASE_INDEX` (strict less); Python `nfeed <= LABEL_BASE_INDEX` (less-or-equal). Off-by-one at exactly LABEL_BASE_INDEX.

484	
485				if (!is_array($items)) {
486					_debug("no articles found.", $debug_enabled);
487	
488					db_query("UPDATE ttrss_feeds
489						SET last_updated = NOW(), last_error = '' WHERE id = '$feed'");
490	
491					return; // no articles
492				}
493	
494				if ($pubsub_state != 2 && PUBSUBHUBBUB_ENABLED) {
495	
496					_debug("checking for PUSH hub...", $debug_enabled);
497	
498					$feed_hub_url = false;
499	
500					$links = $rss->get_links('hub');
501	
502					if ($links && is_array($links)) {
503						foreach ($links as $l) {
504							$feed_hub_url = $l;
505							break;
506						}
507					}
508	
509					_debug("feed hub url: $feed_hub_url", $debug_enabled);
510	
511					$feed_self_url = $fetch_url;
512	
513					$links = $rss->get_links('self');
514	
515					if ($links && is_array($links)) {
516						foreach ($links as $l) {
517							$feed_self_url = $l;
518							break;
519						}
520					}
521	
522					_debug("feed self url = $feed_self_url");
523	
524					if ($feed_hub_url && $feed_self_url && function_exists('curl_init') &&
525						!ini_get("open_basedir")) {
526	
527						require_once 'lib/pubsubhubbub/subscriber.php';
528	
529						$callback_url = get_self_url_prefix() .
530							"/public.php?op=pubsub&id=$feed";
531	
532						$s = new Subscriber($feed_hub_url, $callback_url);
533	
534						$rc = $s->subscribe($feed_self_url);
535	
536						_debug("feed hub url found, subscribe request sent. [rc=$rc]", $debug_enabled);
537	
538						db_query("UPDATE ttrss_feeds SET pubsub_state = 1
539							WHERE id = '$feed'");
540					}
541				}
542	
543				_debug("processing articles...", $debug_enabled);
544	
545				foreach ($items as $item) {
546					if ($_REQUEST['xdebug'] == 3) {
547						print_r($item);
548					}
549

1068						foreach ($filtered_tags as $tag) {

1073							if (!tag_is_valid($tag)) continue;

1075							$result = db_query("SELECT id FROM ttrss_tags

1079								if ($result && db_num_rows($result) == 0) {

1084								}

1089						/* update the cache */

1099						db_query("COMMIT");
1100					}
1101

1121				purge_feed($feed, 0, $debug_enabled);

1153			libxml_use_internal_errors(true);

1155			$charset_hack = '<head>

1157			</head>';

1168	
1169					$local_filename = CACHE_DIR . "/images/" . sha1($src) . ".png";
1170

1176						if ($file_content && strlen($file_content) > 1024) {
1177							file_put_contents($local_filename, $file_content);

19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import hashlib
23	import logging
24	import uuid as _uuid_mod
25	from datetime import datetime, timezone

28	from sqlalchemy import func, select

32	from ttrss.articles.tags import setArticleTags, tag_is_valid
33	from ttrss.models.enclosure import TtRssEnclosure
34	from ttrss.models.entry import TtRssEntry
35	from ttrss.models.user_entry import TtRssUserEntry
36	
37	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
38	
39	# N-gram dedup threshold — rssfuncs.php:_NGRAM_TITLE_DUPLICATE_THRESHOLD
40	_NGRAM_THRESHOLD = 0.5
41	
42	# Filter action type names (from ttrss_filter_actions table)
43	_ACTION_CATCHUP = "catchup"
44	_ACTION_MARK = "mark"
45	_ACTION_TAG = "tag"
46	_ACTION_SCORE = "score"
47	_ACTION_LABEL = "label"
48	_ACTION_STOP = "stop"
49	_ACTION_PUBLISH = "publish"
50	_ACTION_PLUGIN = "plugin"

52	
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# GUID construction
55	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	
57	
58	def _make_guid_from_title(title: str) -> str:
59	    """Fallback GUID from article title hash.
60	
61	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:make_guid_from_title (lines 1401-1404)
62	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php (lines 550-560)
63	    """
64	    return "SHA1:" + hashlib.sha1(title.encode("utf-8", errors="replace")).hexdigest()
65	
66	
67	def build_entry_guid(
68	    entry: Any,
69	    owner_uid: int,
70	) -> str:
71	    """Build a stable, owner-scoped, SHA1-prefixed GUID for a feedparser entry.
72	
73	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php (lines 550-621)
74	    Fallback chain: entry.id → entry.link → title hash.
75	    Owner-scoped: f"{owner_uid},{entry_guid}"
76	    SHA1-prefixed: "SHA1:" + sha1(scoped).hexdigest()
77	    Truncated to 245 chars.
78	    """
79	    raw_guid = (
80	        entry.get("id")
81	        or entry.get("link")
82	        or _make_guid_from_title(entry.get("title", ""))
83	    )
84	    scoped = f"{owner_uid},{raw_guid}"
85	    guid = "SHA1:" + hashlib.sha1(scoped.encode("utf-8", errors="replace")).hexdigest()
86	    return guid[:245]
87	
88	
89	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
90	# Content hash
91	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	
93	
94	def content_hash(content: str) -> str:
95	    """Return SHA1-prefixed hash of article content.
96	
97	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php line 707
98	    """
99	    return "SHA1:" + hashlib.sha1(content.encode("utf-8", errors="replace")).hexdigest()
100	
101	
102	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	# N-gram duplicate detection
104	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	
106	
107	def _is_ngram_duplicate(session: Session, title: str, owner_uid: int) -> bool:
108	    """Check for an existing article with similar title using PostgreSQL similarity().
109	
110	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 867-882
111	    Requires pg_trgm extension (CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm).
112	    Returns True if a similar article already exists.
113	    """
114	    if not title:
115	        return False
116	    try:
117	        result = session.execute(
118	            select(TtRssEntry.id)
119	            .join(TtRssUserEntry, TtRssUserEntry.ref_id == TtRssEntry.id)
120	            .where(TtRssUserEntry.owner_uid == owner_uid)
121	            .where(func.similarity(TtRssEntry.title, title) > _NGRAM_THRESHOLD)
122	            .limit(1)
123	        ).scalar_one_or_none()
124	        return result is not None
125	    except Exception:
126	        # pg_trgm not installed or query failed — skip dedup silently
127	        return False
128	
129	
130	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
131	# Filter action processing
132	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	
134	
135	def apply_filter_actions(
136	    session: Session,
137	    article_id: int,
138	    owner_uid: int,
139	    feed_id: int,
140	    matched_actions: list[dict[str, Any]],
141	    tag_list: list[str],
142	) -> dict[str, Any]:
143	    """Apply filter action dicts to a newly inserted article.
144	
145	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 812-828, 845-863
146	    Returns updated state dict: {unread, marked, published, score, tags}.
147	    """
148	    from ttrss.labels import label_add_article
149	
150	    unread = True
151	    marked = False
152	    published = False
153	    score = 0
154	
155	    for action in matched_actions:
156	        action_type = action.get("type", "")
157	        param = action.get("param", "")
158	
159	        if action_type == _ACTION_CATCHUP:
160	            # Source: rssfuncs.php line 815 — if ($type == "catchup") unread = false
161	            unread = False
162	
163	        elif action_type == _ACTION_MARK:
164	            # Source: rssfuncs.php line 818 — if ($type == "mark") marked = true
165	            marked = True
166	
167	        elif action_type == _ACTION_PUBLISH:
168	            # Source: rssfuncs.php line 821 — if ($type == "publish") published = true
169	            published = True
170	
171	        elif action_type == _ACTION_SCORE:
172	            # Source: rssfuncs.php line 824 — score += (int) param
173	            try:
174	                score += int(param)
175	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
176	                pass
177	
178	        elif action_type == _ACTION_TAG:
179	            # Source: rssfuncs.php line 845 — add tag to tag list
180	            if param and tag_is_valid(param):
181	                tag_list.append(param)
182	
183	        elif action_type == _ACTION_LABEL:
184	            # Source: rssfuncs.php line 851 — assign article to label by caption
185	            if param:
186	                label_add_article(session, article_id, param, owner_uid)
187	
188	        elif action_type == _ACTION_STOP:
189	            # Source: rssfuncs.php line 828 — break out of filter loop
190	            break
191	
192	        # _ACTION_PLUGIN: not wired here (handled by HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER upstream)
193	
194	    return {
195	        "unread": unread,
196	        "marked": marked,
197	        "published": published,
198	        "score": score,
199	        "tags": tag_list,
200	    }
201	
202	
203	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
204	# Enclosure persistence
205	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
206	
207	
208	def persist_enclosures(
209	    session: Session,
210	    article_id: int,
211	    enclosures: list[dict[str, Any]],
212	) -> None:
213	    """Insert enclosures for an article, deduplicated by content_url + post_id.
214	
215	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 982-1020
216	    Skips enclosures with empty content_url.
217	    """
218	    # Load existing URLs to deduplicate
219	    existing_urls = set(
220	        session.execute(
221	            select(TtRssEnclosure.content_url)
222	            .where(TtRssEnclosure.post_id == article_id)
223	        ).scalars().all()
224	    )
225	
226	    for enc in enclosures:
227	        url = enc.get("content_url", "").strip()
228	        if not url or url in existing_urls:
229	            continue
230	        session.add(
231	            TtRssEnclosure(
232	                post_id=article_id,
233	                content_url=url,
234	                content_type=enc.get("content_type", "") or "",
235	                title=enc.get("title", "") or "",
236	                duration=enc.get("duration", "") or "",
237	            )
238	        )
239	        existing_urls.add(url)
240	
241	
242	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
243	# Entry upsert
244	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
245	
246	
247	def upsert_entry(
248	    session: Session,
249	    guid: str,
250	    title: str,
251	    link: str,
252	    content: str,
253	    content_hash_val: str,
254	    author: str,
255	    updated: datetime,
256	    plugin_data: Optional[str] = None,
257	    lang: Optional[str] = None,
258	    num_comments: int = 0,
259	    comments: str = "",
260	) -> tuple[int, bool]:
261	    """INSERT or UPDATE ttrss_entries; return (entry_id, is_new).
262	
263	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 720-750 (INSERT) and 923-970 (UPDATE)
264	    INSERT if GUID not found; UPDATE content/title if content_hash changed.
265	    Returns (entry_id, True) if newly created, (entry_id, False) if existing.
266	    """
267	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
268	
269	    # Try existing entry
270	    existing = session.execute(
271	        select(TtRssEntry.id, TtRssEntry.content_hash)
272	        .where(TtRssEntry.guid == guid)
273	    ).one_or_none()
274	
275	    if existing is not None:
276	        entry_id = existing.id
277	        # Content update detection (rssfuncs.php:923-970)
278	        if existing.content_hash != content_hash_val:
279	            session.execute(
280	                sa_update(TtRssEntry)
281	                .where(TtRssEntry.id == entry_id)
282	                .values(
283	                    title=title,
284	                    content=content,
285	                    content_hash=content_hash_val,
286	                    link=link,
287	                    author=author,
288	                    updated=updated,
289	                    date_updated=now,
290	                    plugin_data=plugin_data,
291	                )
292	            )
293	        return entry_id, False
294	
295	    # New entry
296	    entry = TtRssEntry(
297	        guid=guid,
298	        title=title,
299	        link=link,
300	        content=content,
301	        content_hash=content_hash_val,
302	        author=author,
303	        updated=updated,
304	        date_entered=now,
305	        date_updated=now,
306	        no_orig_date=False,
307	        num_comments=num_comments,
308	        comments=comments,
309	        plugin_data=plugin_data,
310	        lang=lang,
311	    )
312	    session.add(entry)
313	    session.flush()  # populate entry.id
314	    return entry.id, True
315	
316	
317	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
318	# User entry upsert
319	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
320	
321	
322	def upsert_user_entry(
323	    session: Session,
324	    ref_id: int,
325	    feed_id: int,
326	    owner_uid: int,
327	    unread: bool = True,
328	    marked: bool = False,
329	    published: bool = False,
330	    score: int = 0,
331	) -> Optional[int]:
332	    """INSERT into ttrss_user_entries if not exists; return int_id or None.
333	
334	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 887-894
335	    Skips insert if ref_id already has a user_entry for this owner.
336	    Returns int_id of the new row, or None if already existed.
337	    """
338	    existing_int_id = session.execute(
339	        select(TtRssUserEntry.int_id)
340	        .where(TtRssUserEntry.ref_id == ref_id)
341	        .where(TtRssUserEntry.owner_uid == owner_uid)
342	        .limit(1)
343	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
344	
345	    if existing_int_id is not None:
346	        return None  # already exists
347	
348	    user_entry = TtRssUserEntry(
349	        ref_id=ref_id,
350	        feed_id=feed_id,
351	        owner_uid=owner_uid,
352	        unread=unread,
353	        marked=marked,
354	        published=published,
355	        score=score,
356	        uuid=str(_uuid_mod.uuid4()),
357	        tag_cache="",
358	        label_cache="",
359	    )
360	    session.add(user_entry)
361	    session.flush()
362	    return user_entry.int_id
363	
364	
365	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
366	# Top-level persist_article
367	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
368	
369	
370	def persist_article(
371	    session: Session,
372	    entry: Any,
373	    feed_id: int,
374	    owner_uid: int,
375	    filters: list[dict[str, Any]],
376	    enclosures: Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]] = None,
377	) -> bool:
378	    """Persist a single feedparser entry to ttrss_entries + ttrss_user_entries.
379	
380	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:update_rss_feed (lines 545-1117)
381	    Returns True if the article was newly inserted, False if it already existed.
382	    """
383	    from ttrss.articles.filters import (
384	        calculate_article_score,
385	        find_article_filters,
386	        get_article_filters,
387	    )
388	
389	    content = (
390	        entry.get("summary")
391	        or (entry.get("content") or [{}])[0].get("value", "")
392	        or ""
393	    )
394	    title = entry.get("title", "") or ""
395	    link = entry.get("link", "") or ""
396	    author = entry.get("author", "") or ""
397	
398	    # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 636-648 — extract tags from <category> elements
399	    raw_tags = [
400	        t.get("term", "")
401	        for t in (entry.get("tags") or [])
402	        if t.get("term")
403	    ]
404	    tag_list = [t for t in raw_tags if tag_is_valid(t)]
405	
406	    # Source: rssfuncs.php line 650 — parse updated timestamp
407	    updated_parsed = entry.get("updated_parsed") or entry.get("published_parsed")
408	    if updated_parsed:
409	        updated = datetime(*updated_parsed[:6], tzinfo=timezone.utc)
410	    else:
411	        updated = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
412	
413	    # GUID + content hash
414	    guid = build_entry_guid(entry, owner_uid)
415	    ch = content_hash(content)
416	
417	    # N-gram dedup check (rssfuncs.php:867-882)
418	    if _is_ngram_duplicate(session, title, owner_uid):
419	        logger.debug("persist_article: n-gram duplicate detected for %r", title)
420	
421	    # Entry upsert
422	    entry_id, is_new = upsert_entry(
423	        session,
424	        guid=guid,
425	        title=title,
426	        link=link,
427	        content=content,
428	        content_hash_val=ch,
429	        author=author,
430	        updated=updated,
431	        plugin_data=entry.get("plugin_data"),
432	        lang=entry.get("language"),
433	    )
434	
435	    if not is_new:
436	        # Existing entry — no need to apply filters or create user_entry again
437	        return False
438	
439	    # Filter matching
440	    matched = get_article_filters(
441	        filters,
442	        title=title,
443	        content=content,
444	        link=link,
445	        timestamp=None,
446	        author=author,
447	        tags=tag_list,
448	    )
449	
450	    # Additional score from score-type actions (rssfuncs.php:824)
451	    extra_score = calculate_article_score(matched)
452	
453	    # User entry INSERT
454	    int_id = upsert_user_entry(
455	        session,
456	        ref_id=entry_id,
457	        feed_id=feed_id,
458	        owner_uid=owner_uid,
459	        unread=True,
460	        marked=False,
461	        published=False,
462	        score=extra_score,
463	    )
464	
465	    if int_id is None:
466	        return False  # already had a user entry
467	
468	    # Apply filter actions (may add tags, assign labels, change unread/marked/published)
469	    state = apply_filter_actions(
470	        session,
471	        article_id=entry_id,
472	        owner_uid=owner_uid,
473	        feed_id=feed_id,
474	        matched_actions=matched,
475	        tag_list=tag_list,
476	    )
477	
478	    # Update user_entry with filter-determined state
479	    session.execute(
480	        sa_update(TtRssUserEntry)
481	        .where(TtRssUserEntry.int_id == int_id)
482	        .values(
483	            unread=state["unread"],
484	            marked=state["marked"],
485	            published=state["published"],
486	            score=state["score"],
487	        )
488	    )
489	
490	    # Persist tags
491	    if state["tags"]:
492	        setArticleTags(session, entry_id, owner_uid, state["tags"])
493	
494	    # Persist enclosures
495	    if enclosures:
496	        persist_enclosures(session, entry_id, enclosures)
497	
498	    return True
499	
500	
501	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
502	# Label filter helpers
503	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
504	
505	
506	def labels_contains_caption(labels: list, caption: str) -> bool:
507	    """Return True if any label in *labels* has the given caption.
508	
509	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:labels_contains_caption (lines 1381-1386)
510	    PHP: foreach ($labels as $label) { if ($label[1] == $caption) return true; }
511	    """
512	    return any(lbl[1] == caption for lbl in labels if len(lbl) > 1)
513	
514	
515	def assign_article_to_label_filters(
516	    session: "Session",
517	    article_id: int,
518	    filters: list,
519	    owner_uid: int,
520	    article_labels: list,
521	) -> None:
522	    """Assign an article to labels matched by label-type filter actions.
523	
524	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:assign_article_to_label_filters (lines 1391-1400)

527	    """

529	
530	    for action in filters:

532	            cap = action.get("param", "")
533	            if cap and not labels_contains_caption(article_labels, cap):
534	                label_add_article(session, article_id, cap, owner_uid)

1	"""Article filter loading, matching, scoring, and SQL generation.
2	
3	Source: ttrss/include/functions2.php
4	    load_filters        (lines 1491-1563)
5	    filter_to_sql       (lines 2082-2165)
6	
7	        ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php
8	    get_article_filters (lines 1272-1348)
9	    calculate_article_score (lines 1370-1379)
10	    find_article_filter (lines 1350-1357)
11	    find_article_filters (lines 1359-1368)
12	
13	Eliminated (R11): MySQL REGEXP branch — PostgreSQL ~ operator used.
14	Adapted: filter_to_sql returns SQLAlchemy clause element instead of SQL string.
15	"""
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import logging
19	import re
20	from typing import Any, Optional
21	
22	from sqlalchemy import and_, func, not_, or_, select, true
23	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
24	
25	from ttrss.models.entry import TtRssEntry
26	from ttrss.models.feed import TtRssFeed
27	from ttrss.models.filter import TtRssFilter2, TtRssFilter2Action, TtRssFilter2Rule
28	from ttrss.models.user_entry import TtRssUserEntry
29	
30	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
31	
32	# Type alias for a filter dict as returned by load_filters
33	FilterDict = dict[str, Any]
34	
35	
36	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
37	# load_filters
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	
40	
41	def load_filters(
42	    session: Session,
43	    feed_id: int,
44	    owner_uid: int,
45	    action_id: Optional[int] = None,
46	) -> list[FilterDict]:
47	    """Load all enabled filters applicable to a feed, with rules and actions.
48	
49	    Source: ttrss/include/functions2.php:load_filters (lines 1491-1563)
50	    Each returned filter dict has keys: match_any_rule, inverse, rules, actions.
51	    Rules: [{reg_exp, type, inverse}]
52	    Actions: [{type, param}]
53	    """
54	    from ttrss.feeds.categories import getParentCategories
55	    from ttrss.models.category import TtRssFeedCategory
56	    from ttrss.models.filter import TtRssFilterAction, TtRssFilterType
57	
58	    # Get cat_id for this feed
59	    cat_id_row = session.execute(
60	        select(TtRssFeed.cat_id).where(TtRssFeed.id == feed_id)
61	    ).scalar_one_or_none()
62	    cat_id = cat_id_row if cat_id_row is not None else 0
63	
64	    # Build category scope: current cat + all parents
65	    parent_cats = getParentCategories(session, cat_id, owner_uid)
66	    check_cats = parent_cats + [cat_id]
67	
68	    # Load enabled filters ordered by order_id, title
69	    filter_rows = session.execute(
70	        select(
71	            TtRssFilter2.id,
72	            TtRssFilter2.match_any_rule,
73	            TtRssFilter2.inverse,
74	        )
75	        .where(TtRssFilter2.owner_uid == owner_uid)
76	        .where(TtRssFilter2.enabled.is_(True))
77	        .order_by(TtRssFilter2.order_id, TtRssFilter2.title)
78	    ).all()
79	
80	    filters: list[FilterDict] = []
81	
82	    for f_row in filter_rows:
83	        filter_id = f_row.id
84	
85	        # Build rule scope condition
86	        # Source: functions2.php:1516
87	        # (cat_id IS NULL AND cat_filter = false) OR cat_id IN (check_cats)
88	        # With null_cat_qpart added when current feed has no category (cat_id=0)
89	        base_cat_cond = and_(
90	            TtRssFilter2Rule.cat_id.is_(None),
91	            TtRssFilter2Rule.cat_filter.is_(False),
92	        )
93	        if cat_id == 0:
94	            # Feed has no category — also match rules that have cat_id IS NULL (regardless of cat_filter)
95	            cat_cond = TtRssFilter2Rule.cat_id.is_(None)
96	        else:
97	            cat_cond = or_(
98	                base_cat_cond,
99	                TtRssFilter2Rule.cat_id.in_(check_cats),
100	            )
101	
102	        feed_cond = or_(
103	            TtRssFilter2Rule.feed_id.is_(None),
104	            TtRssFilter2Rule.feed_id == feed_id,
105	        )
106	
107	        rule_rows = session.execute(
108	            select(
109	                TtRssFilter2Rule.reg_exp,
110	                TtRssFilter2Rule.inverse,
111	                TtRssFilterType.name.label("type_name"),
112	            )
113	            .join(TtRssFilterType, TtRssFilterType.id == TtRssFilter2Rule.filter_type)
114	            .where(TtRssFilter2Rule.filter_id == filter_id)
115	            .where(cat_cond)
116	            .where(feed_cond)
117	        ).all()
118	
119	        rules = [
120	            {
121	                "reg_exp": r.reg_exp,
122	                "type": r.type_name,
123	                "inverse": bool(r.inverse),
124	            }
125	            for r in rule_rows
126	        ]
127	
128	        action_q = (
129	            select(
130	                TtRssFilter2Action.action_param,
131	                TtRssFilterAction.name.label("type_name"),
132	            )
133	            .join(TtRssFilterAction, TtRssFilterAction.id == TtRssFilter2Action.action_id)
134	            .where(TtRssFilter2Action.filter_id == filter_id)
135	        )
136	        if action_id is not None:
137	            action_q = action_q.where(TtRssFilter2Action.action_id == action_id)
138	
139	        action_rows = session.execute(action_q).all()
140	
141	        actions = [
142	            {"type": a.type_name, "param": a.action_param}
143	            for a in action_rows
144	        ]
145	
146	        if rules and actions:
147	            filters.append(
148	                {
149	                    "match_any_rule": bool(f_row.match_any_rule),
150	                    "inverse": bool(f_row.inverse),
151	                    "rules": rules,
152	                    "actions": actions,
153	                }
154	            )
155	
156	    return filters
157	
158	
159	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
160	# get_article_filters — pure Python regex matching
161	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
162	
163	
164	def get_article_filters(
165	    filters: list[FilterDict],
166	    title: str,
167	    content: str,
168	    link: str,
169	    timestamp: Any,
170	    author: str,
171	    tags: list[str],
172	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
173	    """Apply filter list to an article; return list of matching action dicts.
174	
175	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:get_article_filters (lines 1272-1348)
176	    Adapted: uses Python re.search with IGNORECASE instead of PHP preg_match.
177	    Stops processing after first 'stop' action (matching PHP behaviour).
178	    """
179	    matches: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
180	
181	    # Strip newlines from content for matching (matches PHP's preg_replace)
182	    content_flat = re.sub(r"[\r\n\t]", "", content)
183	
184	    for f in filters:
185	        match_any = f.get("match_any_rule", False)
186	        f_inverse = f.get("inverse", False)
187	        filter_match = False
188	
189	        for rule in f.get("rules", []):
190	            reg_exp = rule.get("reg_exp", "")
191	            if not reg_exp:
192	                continue
193	
194	            # PHP escapes '/' in the pattern; Python re doesn't need this
195	            rule_type = rule.get("type", "")
196	            rule_inverse = rule.get("inverse", False)
197	
198	            try:
199	                match = False
200	                if rule_type == "title":
201	                    match = bool(re.search(reg_exp, title, re.IGNORECASE))
202	                elif rule_type == "content":
203	                    match = bool(re.search(reg_exp, content_flat, re.IGNORECASE))
204	                elif rule_type == "both":
205	                    match = bool(
206	                        re.search(reg_exp, title, re.IGNORECASE)
207	                        or re.search(reg_exp, content_flat, re.IGNORECASE)
208	                    )
209	                elif rule_type == "link":
210	                    match = bool(re.search(reg_exp, link, re.IGNORECASE))
211	                elif rule_type == "author":
212	                    match = bool(re.search(reg_exp, author, re.IGNORECASE))
213	                elif rule_type == "tag":
214	                    match = any(
215	                        re.search(reg_exp, tag, re.IGNORECASE) for tag in tags
216	                    )
217	            except re.error:
218	                # Invalid regex — skip rule (matches PHP @preg_match silent error)
219	                continue
220	
221	            if rule_inverse:
222	                match = not match
223	
224	            if match_any:
225	                if match:
226	                    filter_match = True
227	                    break
228	            else:
229	                filter_match = match
230	                if not match:
231	                    break
232	
233	        if f_inverse:
234	            filter_match = not filter_match
235	
236	        if filter_match:
237	            for action in f.get("actions", []):
238	                matches.append(action)
239	                if action.get("type") == "stop":
240	                    return matches
241	
242	    return matches
243	
244	
245	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
246	# Helper: find actions by type
247	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
248	
249	
250	def find_article_filter(
251	    filters: list[dict[str, Any]], filter_name: str
252	) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
253	    """Return first matching action dict by type name, or None.
254	
255	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:find_article_filter (lines 1350-1357)
256	    """
257	    for f in filters:
258	        if f.get("type") == filter_name:
259	            return f
260	    return None
261	
262	
263	def find_article_filters(
264	    filters: list[dict[str, Any]], filter_name: str
265	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
266	    """Return all action dicts matching the given type name.
267	
268	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:find_article_filters (lines 1359-1368)
269	    """
270	    return [f for f in filters if f.get("type") == filter_name]
271	
272	
273	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
274	# calculate_article_score
275	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
276	
277	
278	def calculate_article_score(filters: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> int:
279	    """Sum all 'score' action params from matched filters.
280	
281	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:calculate_article_score (lines 1370-1379)
282	    """
283	    total = 0
284	    for f in filters:
285	        if f.get("type") == "score":
286	            try:
287	                total += int(f.get("param", 0))
288	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
289	                pass
290	    return total
291	
292	
293	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
294	# filter_to_sql — SQLAlchemy expression generator
295	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
296	
297	
298	def filter_to_sql(
299	    session: Session,
300	    filter_: FilterDict,
301	    owner_uid: int,
302	) -> Any:
303	    """Return a SQLAlchemy clause element for a filter dict.
304	
305	    Source: ttrss/include/functions2.php:filter_to_sql (lines 2082-2165)
306	    Adapted: returns SQLAlchemy expression instead of raw SQL string.
307	    PostgreSQL `~*` (case-insensitive POSIX regex) used instead of PHP LOWER ~ LOWER.
308	    Eliminated: MySQL REGEXP branch (R11).
309	    """
310	    from ttrss.feeds.categories import getChildCategories
311	
312	    rule_clauses = []
313	
314	    for rule in filter_.get("rules", []):
315	        reg_exp = rule.get("reg_exp", "")
316	        if not reg_exp:
317	            continue
318	
319	        # Validate regex
320	        try:
321	            re.compile(reg_exp)
322	        except re.error:
323	            continue
324	
325	        rule_type = rule.get("type", "")
326	
327	        # PostgreSQL ~* is case-insensitive POSIX regex (equivalent to PHP LOWER ~ LOWER)
328	        if rule_type == "title":
329	            qpart = TtRssEntry.title.op("~*")(reg_exp)
330	        elif rule_type == "content":
331	            qpart = TtRssEntry.content.op("~*")(reg_exp)
332	        elif rule_type == "both":
333	            qpart = or_(
334	                TtRssEntry.title.op("~*")(reg_exp),
335	                TtRssEntry.content.op("~*")(reg_exp),
336	            )
337	        elif rule_type == "tag":
338	            qpart = TtRssUserEntry.tag_cache.op("~*")(reg_exp)
339	        elif rule_type == "link":
340	            qpart = TtRssEntry.link.op("~*")(reg_exp)
341	        elif rule_type == "author":
342	            qpart = TtRssEntry.author.op("~*")(reg_exp)
343	        else:
344	            continue
345	
346	        if rule.get("inverse"):
347	            qpart = not_(qpart)
348	
349	        # Rule-level feed scope
350	        rule_feed_id = rule.get("feed_id")
351	        if rule_feed_id and int(rule_feed_id) > 0:
352	            qpart = and_(qpart, TtRssUserEntry.feed_id == int(rule_feed_id))
353	
354	        # Rule-level category scope
355	        rule_cat_id = rule.get("cat_id")
356	        if rule_cat_id is not None:
357	            if int(rule_cat_id) > 0:
358	                children = getChildCategories(session, int(rule_cat_id), owner_uid)
359	                children.append(int(rule_cat_id))
360	                qpart = and_(qpart, TtRssFeed.cat_id.in_(children))
361	            else:
362	                qpart = and_(qpart, TtRssFeed.cat_id.is_(None))
363	
364	        # Only applies to entries with a feed (not archived)
365	        qpart = and_(qpart, TtRssUserEntry.feed_id.isnot(None))
366	
367	        rule_clauses.append(qpart)
368	
369	    if not rule_clauses:
370	        return false_clause()
371	
372	    match_any = filter_.get("match_any_rule", False)
373	    full_clause = or_(*rule_clauses) if match_any else and_(*rule_clauses)
374	
375	    if filter_.get("inverse"):
376	        full_clause = not_(full_clause)
377	
378	    return full_clause
379	
380	
381	def false_clause() -> Any:
382	    """Return a SQLAlchemy expression that is always false."""
383	    from sqlalchemy import literal
384	    return literal(False)
385

92	
93	    Source: ttrss/include/functions.php:purge_feed (lines 209-291)

I now have a complete picture of all discrepancies. Let me fix them systematically, starting with `persist.py` then `feed_tasks.py`.

**Discrepancies found in Pipeline 1 audit:**
1. **D17** (CRITICAL): Content priority inverted — PHP uses `get_content()` first (full body), Python uses `summary` first
2. **D33** (HIGH): "filter" action type not checked — articles that should be discarded are persisted
3. **D30** (MEDIUM): `score >= -500` threshold for unread not implemented
4. **D37** (MEDIUM): N-gram duplicate should set `unread=False`, not just log
5. **D34** (MEDIUM): `last_marked`/`last_published` never set on user_entry INSERT
6. **D23** (MEDIUM): HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER dict missing `stored` and `feed` keys
7. **D24** (MEDIUM): `mark_unread_on_update` feed preference not implemented
8. **D34** (MEDIUM): `purge_feed` not called after article processing

Fixing now:

Status: Completed

```
def persist_article(
    session: Session,
    entry: Any,
    feed_id: int,
    owner_uid: int,
    filters: list[dict[str, Any]],
    enclosures: Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]] = None,
    mark_unread_on_update: bool = False,
) -> bool:
    """Persist a single feedparser entry to ttrss_entries + ttrss_user_entries.

    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:update_rss_feed (lines 545-1117)
    Returns True if the article was newly inserted, False if it already existed.
    """
    from ttrss.articles.filters import (
        calculate_article_score,
        find_article_filter,
        find_article_filters,
        get_article_filters,
    )

    # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 589-590 — get_content() first (full body), get_description() fallback
    # PHP FeedParser: get_content() = full body; get_description() = summary/description
    # feedparser: entry.content[0].value = full body; entry.summary = description
    content = (
        (entry.get("content") or [{}])[0].get("value", "")
        or entry.get("summary", "")
        or ""
    )
```

    # Entry upsert
    entry_id, is_new = upsert_entry(
        session,
        guid=guid,
        title=title,
        link=link,
        content=content,
        content_hash_val=ch,
        author=author,
        updated=updated,
        plugin_data=entry.get("plugin_data"),
        lang=entry.get("language"),
        mark_unread_on_update=mark_unread_on_update,
    )

    if not is_new:
        # Existing entry — no need to apply filters or create user_entry again
        return False

    # Filter matching
    matched = get_article_filters(
        filters,
        title=title,
        content=content,
        link=link,
        timestamp=None,
        author=author,
        tags=tag_list,
    )

    # Source: rssfuncs.php line 823 — "filter" action type discards article before INSERT
    if find_article_filter(matched, "filter"):
        return False

    # Additional score from score-type actions (rssfuncs.php:824)
    extra_score = calculate_article_score(matched)

    # Source: rssfuncs.php line 845 — unread=false if score < -500 or catchup action
    # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 867-882 — n-gram duplicate → mark read (unread=false)
    ngram_dup = _is_ngram_duplicate(session, title, owner_uid)
    if ngram_dup:
        logger.debug("persist_article: n-gram duplicate detected for %r", title)

    # User entry INSERT
    int_id = upsert_user_entry(
        session,
        ref_id=entry_id,
        feed_id=feed_id,
        owner_uid=owner_uid,
        unread=True,
        marked=False,
        published=False,
        score=extra_score,
    )

    if int_id is None:
        return False  # already had a user entry

    # Apply filter actions (may add tags, assign labels, change unread/marked/published)
    state = apply_filter_actions(
        session,
        article_id=entry_id,
        owner_uid=owner_uid,
        feed_id=feed_id,
        matched_actions=matched,
        tag_list=tag_list,
    )

    # Source: rssfuncs.php line 845 — score < -500 forces read regardless of catchup action
    if extra_score < -500:
        state["unread"] = False

    # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 867-882 — n-gram title duplicate → insert as read
    if ngram_dup:
        state["unread"] = False

    # Update user_entry with filter-determined state
    session.execute(
        sa_update(TtRssUserEntry)
        .where(TtRssUserEntry.int_id == int_id)
        .values(
            unread=state["unread"],
            marked=state["marked"],
            published=state["published"],
            score=state["score"],
        )
    )

**Tool Call: Edit target\-repos/ttrss\-python/ttrss/articles/persist.py**
Status: Completed

Diff: /Users/rd/devel/Capgemini/Capgemini_Internal/OSS_Migration/target-repos/ttrss-python/ttrss/articles/persist.py
```
def upsert_entry(
    session: Session,
    guid: str,
    title: str,
    link: str,
    content: str,
    content_hash_val: str,
    author: str,
    updated: datetime,
    plugin_data: Optional[str] = None,
    lang: Optional[str] = None,
    num_comments: int = 0,
    comments: str = "",
    mark_unread_on_update: bool = False,
) -> tuple[int, bool]:
    """INSERT or UPDATE ttrss_entries; return (entry_id, is_new).

    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 720-750 (INSERT) and 923-970 (UPDATE)
    INSERT if GUID not found; UPDATE content/title if content_hash changed.
    Returns (entry_id, True) if newly created, (entry_id, False) if existing.
    Note: rssfuncs.php lines 926-944 — num_comments, plugin_data, and title changes
          also trigger $post_needs_update. Python only checks content_hash for
          simplicity; those fields are updated along with content when hash changes.
    """
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    # Try existing entry
    existing = session.execute(
        select(TtRssEntry.id, TtRssEntry.content_hash)
        .where(TtRssEntry.guid == guid)
    ).one_or_none()

    if existing is not None:
        entry_id = existing.id
        # Content update detection (rssfuncs.php:923-970)
        if existing.content_hash != content_hash_val:
            session.execute(
                sa_update(TtRssEntry)
                .where(TtRssEntry.id == entry_id)
                .values(
                    title=title,
                    content=content,
                    content_hash=content_hash_val,
                    link=link,
                    author=author,
                    updated=updated,
                    date_updated=now,
                    plugin_data=plugin_data,
                )
            )
            # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 964-968 — if content changed significantly
            # and mark_unread_on_update is set, re-mark all user entries as unread
            if mark_unread_on_update:
                session.execute(
                    sa_update(TtRssUserEntry)
                    .where(TtRssUserEntry.ref_id == entry_id)
                    .values(last_read=None, unread=True)
                )
        return entry_id, False
```

        from sqlalchemy import select as sa_select
        from ttrss.articles.persist import build_entry_guid
        from ttrss.models.entry import TtRssEntry as _TtRssEntry

        for entry in parsed.entries:
            # Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 589-590 — get_content() first (full body),
            # get_description() fallback (summary/description).

            content = (
                (entry.get("content") or [{}])[0].get("value", "")
                or entry.get("summary", "")
            )

            # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 658-671 — lookup existing entry for HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER
            # "stored" key gives plugins access to the previously stored title/content/link/author;
            # "feed" key gives plugins access to feed metadata (id, fetch_url, site_url).
            entry_guid = build_entry_guid(entry, feed.owner_uid)
            stored_row = db.session.execute(
                sa_select(
                    _TtRssEntry.plugin_data,
                    _TtRssEntry.title,
                    _TtRssEntry.content,
                    _TtRssEntry.link,
                    _TtRssEntry.author,
                ).where(_TtRssEntry.guid == entry_guid)
            ).one_or_none()
            entry_plugin_data = ""
            stored_article: dict = {}
            if stored_row is not None:
                entry_plugin_data = stored_row.plugin_data or ""
                stored_article = {
                    "title": stored_row.title or "",
                    "content": stored_row.content or "",
                    "link": stored_row.link or "",
                    "author": stored_row.author or "",

                "guid": entry.get("id", ""),  # Source: rssfuncs.php line 674 — "guid" => $entry_guid

                "link": entry.get("link", ""),  # Source: rssfuncs.php line 677 — "link" => $entry_link
                "tags": [],  # Source: rssfuncs.php line 678 — "tags" => $entry_tags
                "plugin_data": entry_plugin_data,  # Source: rssfuncs.php line 679 — "plugin_data" => $entry_plugin_data (DB value if existing)
                "author": entry.get("author", ""),  # Source: rssfuncs.php line 680 — "author" => $entry_author
                "stored": stored_article,  # Source: rssfuncs.php line 681 — "stored" => $stored_article
                "feed": {  # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 682-685 — "feed" => array(...)

                    "fetch_url": feed.feed_url,
                    "site_url": feed.site_url,
                },
            }

391	def test_update_feed_plugin_provides_feed_data():
392	    """HOOK_FETCH_FEED returning non-None skips HTTP fetch."""
393	    from ttrss.tasks.feed_tasks import update_feed
394	
395	    feed = _make_feed()
396	    session = MagicMock()
397	    session.get.return_value = feed
398	    mock_app, _ = _make_app_ctx(session)
399	    pm_mock = MagicMock()
400	    pm_mock.hook.hook_fetch_feed.return_value = [b"<rss/>"]  # plugin provides data
401	    pm_mock.hook.hook_feed_fetched.return_value = []
402	    pm_mock.hook.hook_feed_parsed.return_value = []
403	    pm_mock.hook.hook_article_filter.return_value = []
404	
405	    parsed = MagicMock()
406	    parsed.bozo = False
407	    parsed.entries = []
408	
409	    with patch("ttrss.create_app", return_value=mock_app), \
410	         patch("ttrss.extensions.db") as mock_db, \
411	         patch("ttrss.plugins.manager.get_plugin_manager", return_value=pm_mock), \
412	         patch("ttrss.tasks.feed_tasks.asyncio") as mock_asyncio, \
413	         patch("ttrss.tasks.feed_tasks.feedparser.parse", return_value=parsed), \
414	         patch("ttrss.articles.filters.load_filters", return_value=[]):
415	        mock_db.session = session
416	        result = update_feed.run(5)
417	
418	    # asyncio.run should NOT be called — plugin bypassed HTTP
419	    mock_asyncio.run.assert_not_called()

# Semantic Verification Report

**Methodology**: ADR-0016 — 40-category taxonomy (D01-D40), 8 integration pipelines, complexity-tiered triage.
**Spec reference**: `specs/14-semantic-discrepancies.md`
**Success criteria**: Zero unfixed discrepancies + all baseline pytest tests pass.

---

## Status Summary

| Phase | Scope | Started | Complete |
|-------|-------|---------|---------|
| A — Tier 1 deep audit | 52 high-risk functions | 2026-04-05 | In progress |
| B — Integration pipelines | 8 pipelines | — | Not started |
| C — Tier 2 standard audit | ~150 functions | — | Not started |
| D — Tier 3 quick check | ~270 functions | — | Not started |
| E — Model deep check | 37 ORM classes | — | Not started |
| F — Cross-workstream sweeps | Systemic patterns | — | Not started |

---

## Phase A — Tier 1 Deep Audit

### WS-06: Feed Update Pipeline

#### `update_rss_feed` / `update_feed` + `persist_article` + `upsert_entry`

PHP source: `ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:update_rss_feed` (lines 203–1149)
Python: `ttrss/tasks/feed_tasks.py:update_feed` (lines 226–453) + `ttrss/articles/persist.py`
Audit date: 2026-04-05
Auditor: semantic verification Phase A

---

#### Discrepancy Log

| # | D-code | Severity | PHP Location | PHP Behavior | Python Location | Python Behavior | Status |
|---|--------|----------|-------------|--------------|-----------------|-----------------|--------|
| 1 | D17 | CRITICAL | rssfuncs.php:589-590 | `get_content()` first (full body); `get_description()` fallback (summary) | feed_tasks.py:382 + persist.py:389 | `summary` first; `content` fallback — **inverted** | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) |
| 2 | D33 | HIGH | rssfuncs.php:823-826 | "filter" action type → `continue` (article discarded before INSERT) | persist.py | "filter" action not checked — article persisted | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) |
| 3 | D08 | HIGH | rssfuncs.php:709,973,1001,1022,1066,1099 | 3 `BEGIN/COMMIT` cycles per article (entry INSERT, enclosures, tags) | feed_tasks.py:438 | Single `commit()` after full feed loop — all-or-nothing per feed | **DOCUMENTED** — structural redesign; per-feed commit is intentional for Celery task atomicity |
| 4 | D30 | MEDIUM | rssfuncs.php:845 | `unread=false` if `score < -500` OR catchup action | persist.py | Score threshold not checked; only catchup action sets unread=False | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) |
| 5 | D37 | MEDIUM | rssfuncs.php:867-882 | N-gram title duplicate → `$unread = 'false'` (inserted as read) | persist.py:418-419 | N-gram duplicate → log only; unread stays True | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) |
| 6 | D23 | MEDIUM | rssfuncs.php:658-685 | HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER article dict includes `"stored"` (prev DB values) + `"feed"` (id/fetch_url/site_url) | feed_tasks.py:388-397 | "stored" and "feed" keys absent — plugins that read them silently fail | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) |
| 7 | D34 | MEDIUM | rssfuncs.php:884-885 | `last_marked=NOW()` if marked; `last_published=NOW()` if published on user_entry INSERT | persist.py:upsert_user_entry | `last_marked`/`last_published` always NULL on INSERT | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) |
| 8 | D24 | MEDIUM | rssfuncs.php:964-968 | If content changed significantly AND `mark_unread_on_update` feed pref: `UPDATE user_entries SET unread=true, last_read=NULL` | persist.py:upsert_entry | Not implemented | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) |
| 9 | D34 | MEDIUM | rssfuncs.php:1121 | `purge_feed($feed, 0)` called after processing all articles | feed_tasks.py | Not called — old articles accumulate indefinitely | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) |
| 10 | D23 | MEDIUM | rssfuncs.php:697 | Plugin-filtered `$entry_content` (post-HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER) stored in DB | feed_tasks.py:448 | Raw feedparser entry content stored; plugin modifications lost | **FIXED** (2026-04-05) — `entry_copy["content"]` overwritten with plugin-filtered value |
| 11 | D11 | LOW | rssfuncs.php:804-808 | `ALLOW_DUPLICATE_POSTS` pref → adds `AND (feed_id='$feed' OR feed_id IS NULL)` to user_entry lookup | persist.py:upsert_user_entry | Always global dedup regardless of preference | **DOCUMENTED** — not yet implemented |
| 12 | D19 | LOW | rssfuncs.php:623-624 | `$entry_author`, `$entry_comments` truncated to 245 chars | persist.py | author not truncated (GUID correctly truncated in build_entry_guid to 245) | **DOCUMENTED** — SHA1 GUID is always 45 chars; author truncation low risk with pg varchar |
| 13 | D37 | LOW | rssfuncs.php | `$date_feed_processed = date('Y-m-d H:i')` set once; all articles in batch share same `date_entered` | persist.py:upsert_entry:304 | Each article gets independent `datetime.now()` — microsecond variation within batch | **DOCUMENTED** — behavioral difference, low impact on user-visible behavior |
| 14 | D29 | LOW | rssfuncs.php:926-933 | `num_comments` OR `plugin_data` changes (not just content_hash) trigger `$post_needs_update` | persist.py:upsert_entry | Only `content_hash` mismatch triggers update | **DOCUMENTED** — rare edge case; content_hash catches main update scenario |
| 15 | D34 | ACKNOWLEDGED | rssfuncs.php:424-457 | Favicon refresh + average color calculation per feed | feed_tasks.py | Not implemented (Note in code) | ACKNOWLEDGED — feature elimination, Phase 3+ |
| 16 | D34 | ACKNOWLEDGED | rssfuncs.php:494-541 | PubSubHubbub hub subscription | feed_tasks.py | Not implemented (Note in code) | ACKNOWLEDGED — feature elimination |
| 17 | D34 | ACKNOWLEDGED | rssfuncs.php:459-474 | Feed title + site_url updated from parsed RSS metadata | feed_tasks.py | Not implemented (Note in code) | ACKNOWLEDGED — deferred |
| 18 | D34 | ACKNOWLEDGED | rssfuncs.php:380-385 | Language detection (`Text_LanguageDetect`) per article | feed_tasks.py | Not implemented (Note in code) | ACKNOWLEDGED — feature elimination |
| 19 | D34 | ACKNOWLEDGED | rssfuncs.php:702-703 | `cache_images()` per article | feed_tasks.py | Not implemented (Note in code) | ACKNOWLEDGED — feature elimination |
| 20 | D38 | DOCUMENTED | rssfuncs.php:296-301 | Conditional GET via `If-Modified-Since` with `$last_article_timestamp` (epoch of most recent article) | feed_tasks.py | ETag/Last-Modified from response headers (ADR-0015 structural redesign) | DOCUMENTED — different mechanism, equivalent intent; ADR-0015 |
| 21 | D34 | ACKNOWLEDGED | rssfuncs.php:273-341 | XML disk cache (30-second cache for unauthenticated feeds, SHA1 filename) | feed_tasks.py | Not implemented | ACKNOWLEDGED — performance feature; no behavioral impact on correctness |

---

#### Fix Details

**Fix 1 — D17 Content priority** (`persist.py:389-392`, `feed_tasks.py:382-384`):
```python
# Before (wrong — summary first):
content = entry.get("summary") or (entry.get("content") or [{}])[0].get("value", "")
# After (correct — full content first, summary fallback):
content = (entry.get("content") or [{}])[0].get("value", "") or entry.get("summary", "")
```
Impact: Every article with both `<content>` and `<description>` elements showed wrong content.

**Fix 2 — D33 "filter" action** (`persist.py` after `get_article_filters`):
```python
if find_article_filter(matched, "filter"):
    return False  # discard article — PHP: db_query("COMMIT"); continue
```
Impact: Articles matching a "filter" action filter were being inserted when they should be discarded.

**Fix 3 — D30 Score threshold** (`persist.py` after `calculate_article_score`):
```python
if extra_score < -500:
    state["unread"] = False
```
Impact: Articles with score < -500 were inserted as unread when they should be inserted as read.

**Fix 4 — D37 N-gram unread** (`persist.py`):
```python
ngram_dup = _is_ngram_duplicate(session, title, owner_uid)
# ... later:
if ngram_dup:
    state["unread"] = False
```
Impact: N-gram duplicates inserted as unread instead of read.

**Fix 5 — D23 HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER dict** (`feed_tasks.py`):
Added `"stored"` (previous DB values) and `"feed"` (id/fetch_url/site_url) keys to article dict. Added pre-entry GUID lookup to populate stored_article from DB.

**Fix 6 — D34 last_marked/last_published** (`persist.py:upsert_user_entry`):
```python
last_marked=datetime.now(timezone.utc) if marked else None,
last_published=datetime.now(timezone.utc) if published else None,
```

**Fix 7 — D24 mark_unread_on_update** (`persist.py:upsert_entry`):
When `content_hash` changes and `mark_unread_on_update=True`:
```python
session.execute(sa_update(TtRssUserEntry).where(...).values(last_read=None, unread=True))
```
Threaded through `persist_article(mark_unread_on_update=feed.mark_unread_on_update)` from `update_feed`.

**Fix 8 — purge_feed** (`feed_tasks.py`):
```python
from ttrss.feeds.ops import purge_feed
purge_feed(db.session, feed_id)
```
Called after article loop, before `last_updated` stamp. `purge_feed` already existed in `feeds/ops.py`.

**Fix 9 — Plugin-filtered content propagation** (`feed_tasks.py`):
```python
entry_copy["content"] = [{"value": content, "type": "text/html"}]
```
Overwrites raw feedparser content structure with plugin-filtered content so `persist_article` stores correct value.

---

#### Test results after fixes

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Unit tests passing | 598 | 598 |
| Integration tests | Pre-existing failure (login test) | Unchanged |
| New test failures | — | 0 |

---

**Tool Call: git add target-repos/ttrss-python/ttrss/articles/persist.py target-repos/ttrss-python/ttrss/tasks/feed_tasks.py target-repos/ttrss-python/tests/unit/test_tasks_feed_tasks.py docs/semantic_verification_report.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Phase A Tier 1: audit update_rss_feed pipeline, fix 9 semantic discrepancies

Findings from line-by-line PHP→Python comparison of update_rss_feed (rssfuncs.php:203-1149)
against update_feed + persist_article + upsert_entry using D01-D40 taxonomy (ADR-0016).

Fixes applied:
- D17: Content priority inverted — PHP uses get_content() first (full body), get_description()
  fallback. Python had summary first. Fixed in both feed_tasks.py and persist.py.
- D33: "filter" action type not checked in persist_article — articles that should be
  discarded were being inserted. Added find_article_filter("filter") early return.
- D30: score < -500 threshold for unread not implemented. Added check after
  calculate_article_score so articles with very low score are inserted as read.
- D37: N-gram duplicate → unread=False (PHP behavior); Python only logged. Fixed.
- D23: HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER article dict missing "stored" (prev DB values) and "feed"
  (id/fetch_url/site_url) keys. Plugins reading these silently failed. Added pre-entry
  GUID lookup to populate stored_article from DB.
- D34: last_marked/last_published always NULL on user_entry INSERT. Now set to NOW()
  when marked/published are True (rssfuncs.php:884-885).
- D24: mark_unread_on_update feed preference not implemented in upsert_entry. Now when
  content_hash changes and mark_unread_on_update=True, user_entries set unread=True.
  Threaded through persist_article from update_feed.
- D34: purge_feed() not called after article processing loop. Added call to existing
  feeds/ops.py:purge_feed() after entries loop (rssfuncs.php:1121).
- Plugin-filtered content not propagated to persist_article. entry_copy["content"] now
  overwritten with plugin-filtered value before persist_article call.

Test updates: 4 feed_tasks unit tests updated to mock purge_feed (new call) and
build_entry_guid (new per-entry DB lookup for stored_article).

Result: 598 unit tests pass (no regressions).
Documented in: docs/semantic_verification_report.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"**

### Phase A Progress (commit 27f774b — 2026-04-05)

**WS-06 update_rss_feed audit COMPLETE** — 9 discrepancies found and fixed:
- D17 FIXED: Content priority inverted (summary→content, should be content→summary)
- D33 FIXED: "filter" action type not checked in persist_article (articles not discarded)
- D30 FIXED: score < -500 threshold for unread not implemented
- D37 FIXED: N-gram duplicate → unread should be False, was only logging
- D23 FIXED: HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER article dict missing "stored" + "feed" keys
- D34 FIXED: last_marked/last_published always NULL on user_entry INSERT
- D24 FIXED: mark_unread_on_update feed preference not wired through
- D34 FIXED: purge_feed() not called after article loop
- D34 FIXED: plugin-filtered content not propagated to persist_article
- 598 tests pass. Report: `docs/semantic_verification_report.md`

**DOCUMENTED (not fixed)**: Per-article transaction granularity (D08), ALLOW_DUPLICATE_POSTS (D11),
author truncation (D19), date_entered batch timestamp (D37), num_comments/plugin_data update triggers (D29).
Feature eliminations acknowledged: favicon, PubSubHubbub, language detection, image cache, XML disk cache.

## Assistant

**Phase A — WS-06 audit complete.** Here's what was done:

---

**21 discrepancies catalogued** across `update_feed` / `persist_article` / `upsert_entry`:

**9 fixed (commit `27f774b`):**

| Fix | Impact |
|-----|--------|
| **D17** Content priority inverted | Every article with both `<content>` and `<description>` showed wrong text |
| **D33** "filter" action not checked | Articles matching discard-filters were being inserted |
| **D30** `score < -500` threshold missing | Low-scored articles inserted as unread instead of read |
| **D37** N-gram dedup didn't affect unread | Duplicates inserted as unread instead of read |
| **D23** `stored`/`feed` keys missing from HOOK_ARTICLE_FILTER dict | Plugins using prev-stored values silently failed |
| **D34** `last_marked`/`last_published` always NULL | User entry state incorrect after filter-based marking |
| **D24** `mark_unread_on_update` not wired | Feed preference had no effect |
| **D34** `purge_feed` not called | Old articles accumulated indefinitely |
| Content propagation | Plugin-filtered content was discarded — raw feedparser content stored instead |

**4 documented (not fixed):** per-article transaction granularity (structural redesign), `ALLOW_DUPLICATE_POSTS` pref, author truncation, `num_comments`/`plugin_data` update triggers.

**7 acknowledged eliminations:** favicon, PubSubHubbub, language detection, image cache, XML disk cache, feed title update, site_url update.

**598 unit tests pass.** Next: `dispatch_feed_updates`, then Pipeline 2 (`queryFeedHeadlines`, `search_to_sql`).

48		/**
49		 * Update a feed batch.
50		 * Used by daemons to update n feeds by run.
51		 * Only update feed needing a update, and not being processed
52		 * by another process.
53		 *
54		 * @param mixed $link Database link
55		 * @param integer $limit Maximum number of feeds in update batch. Default to DAEMON_FEED_LIMIT.
56		 * @param boolean $from_http Set to true if you call this function from http to disable cli specific code.
57		 * @param boolean $debug Set to false to disable debug output. Default to true.
58		 * @return void
59		 */
60		function update_daemon_common($limit = DAEMON_FEED_LIMIT, $from_http = false, $debug = true) {
61			// Process all other feeds using last_updated and interval parameters
62	
63			$schema_version = get_schema_version();
64	
65			if ($schema_version != SCHEMA_VERSION) {
66				die("Schema version is wrong, please upgrade the database.\n");
67			}
68	
69			define('PREFS_NO_CACHE', true);
70	
71			// Test if the user has loggued in recently. If not, it does not update its feeds.
72			if (!SINGLE_USER_MODE && DAEMON_UPDATE_LOGIN_LIMIT > 0) {
73				if (DB_TYPE == "pgsql") {
74					$login_thresh_qpart = "AND ttrss_users.last_login >= NOW() - INTERVAL '".DAEMON_UPDATE_LOGIN_LIMIT." days'";
75				} else {
76					$login_thresh_qpart = "AND ttrss_users.last_login >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL ".DAEMON_UPDATE_LOGIN_LIMIT." DAY)";
77				}
78			} else {
79				$login_thresh_qpart = "";
80			}
81	
82			// Test if the feed need a update (update interval exceeded).
83			if (DB_TYPE == "pgsql") {
84				$update_limit_qpart = "AND ((
85						ttrss_feeds.update_interval = 0
86						AND ttrss_user_prefs.value != '-1'
87						AND ttrss_feeds.last_updated < NOW() - CAST((ttrss_user_prefs.value || ' minutes') AS INTERVAL)
88					) OR (
89						ttrss_feeds.update_interval > 0
90						AND ttrss_feeds.last_updated < NOW() - CAST((ttrss_feeds.update_interval || ' minutes') AS INTERVAL)
91					) OR (ttrss_feeds.last_updated IS NULL
92						AND ttrss_user_prefs.value != '-1')
93					OR (last_updated = '1970-01-01 00:00:00'
94						AND ttrss_user_prefs.value != '-1'))";
95			} else {
96				$update_limit_qpart = "AND ((
97						ttrss_feeds.update_interval = 0
98						AND ttrss_user_prefs.value != '-1'
99						AND ttrss_feeds.last_updated < DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL CONVERT(ttrss_user_prefs.value, SIGNED INTEGER) MINUTE)
100					) OR (
101						ttrss_feeds.update_interval > 0
102						AND ttrss_feeds.last_updated < DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL ttrss_feeds.update_interval MINUTE)
103					) OR (ttrss_feeds.last_updated IS NULL
104						AND ttrss_user_prefs.value != '-1')
105					OR (last_updated = '1970-01-01 00:00:00'
106						AND ttrss_user_prefs.value != '-1'))";
107			}
108	
109			// Test if feed is currently being updated by another process.
110			if (DB_TYPE == "pgsql") {
111				$updstart_thresh_qpart = "AND (ttrss_feeds.last_update_started IS NULL OR ttrss_feeds.last_update_started < NOW() - INTERVAL '10 minutes')";
112			} else {
113				$updstart_thresh_qpart = "AND (ttrss_feeds.last_update_started IS NULL OR ttrss_feeds.last_update_started < DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 10 MINUTE))";
114			}
115	
116			// Test if there is a limit to number of updated feeds
117			$query_limit = "";
118			if($limit) $query_limit = sprintf("LIMIT %d", $limit);
119	
120			$query = "SELECT DISTINCT ttrss_feeds.feed_url, ttrss_feeds.last_updated
121				FROM
122					ttrss_feeds, ttrss_users, ttrss_user_prefs
123				WHERE
124					ttrss_feeds.owner_uid = ttrss_users.id
125					AND ttrss_user_prefs.profile IS NULL
126					AND ttrss_users.id = ttrss_user_prefs.owner_uid
127					AND ttrss_user_prefs.pref_name = 'DEFAULT_UPDATE_INTERVAL'
128					$login_thresh_qpart $update_limit_qpart
129					$updstart_thresh_qpart
130					ORDER BY last_updated $query_limit";
131	
132			// We search for feed needing update.
133			$result = db_query($query);
134	
135			if($debug) _debug(sprintf("Scheduled %d feeds to update...", db_num_rows($result)));
136	
137			// Here is a little cache magic in order to minimize risk of double feed updates.
138			$feeds_to_update = array();
139			while ($line = db_fetch_assoc($result)) {
140				array_push($feeds_to_update, db_escape_string($line['feed_url']));
141			}
142	
143			// We update the feed last update started date before anything else.
144			// There is no lag due to feed contents downloads
145			// It prevent an other process to update the same feed.
146	
147			if(count($feeds_to_update) > 0) {
148				$feeds_quoted = array();
149	
150				foreach ($feeds_to_update as $feed) {
151					array_push($feeds_quoted, "'" . db_escape_string($feed) . "'");
152				}
153	
154				db_query(sprintf("UPDATE ttrss_feeds SET last_update_started = NOW()
155					WHERE feed_url IN (%s)", implode(',', $feeds_quoted)));
156			}
157	
158			$nf = 0;
159	
160			// For each feed, we call the feed update function.
161			foreach ($feeds_to_update as $feed) {
162				if($debug) _debug("Base feed: $feed");
163	
164				//update_rss_feed($line["id"], true);
165	
166				// since we have the data cached, we can deal with other feeds with the same url
167	
168				$tmp_result = db_query("SELECT DISTINCT ttrss_feeds.id,last_updated,ttrss_feeds.owner_uid
169				FROM ttrss_feeds, ttrss_users, ttrss_user_prefs WHERE
170					ttrss_user_prefs.owner_uid = ttrss_feeds.owner_uid AND
171					ttrss_users.id = ttrss_user_prefs.owner_uid AND
172					ttrss_user_prefs.pref_name = 'DEFAULT_UPDATE_INTERVAL' AND
173					ttrss_user_prefs.profile IS NULL AND
174					feed_url = '".db_escape_string($feed)."' AND
175					(ttrss_feeds.update_interval > 0 OR
176						ttrss_user_prefs.value != '-1')
177					$login_thresh_qpart
178				ORDER BY ttrss_feeds.id $query_limit");
179	
180				if (db_num_rows($tmp_result) > 0) {
181					$rss = false;
182	
183					while ($tline = db_fetch_assoc($tmp_result)) {
184						if($debug) _debug(" => " . $tline["last_updated"] . ", " . $tline["id"] . " " . $tline["owner_uid"]);
185	
186						$rss = update_rss_feed($tline["id"], true, false);
187						_debug_suppress(false);
188						++$nf;
189					}
190				}
191			}
192	
193			require_once "digest.php";
194	
195			// Send feed digests by email if needed.
196			send_headlines_digests($debug);
197	
198			return $nf;
199

53	def dispatch_feed_updates(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
54	    """
55	    Beat-triggered dispatcher: query due feeds, fan-out update_feed per feed.
56	    Source: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php:update_daemon_common (lines 120-192)
57	    Note: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 93-94 — PHP checks last_updated = '1970-01-01 00:00:00'
58	          as a sentinel value for feeds that have never been updated.  Python uses IS NULL instead,
59	          relying on the ORM schema default; the 1970 sentinel is not reproduced.
60	    Note: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 72-80 — PHP applies DAEMON_UPDATE_LOGIN_LIMIT to cap
61	          the number of active users processed per cycle.  Python omits this per-login throttle;
62	          all due feeds up to DAEMON_FEED_LIMIT are dispatched unconditionally.
63	    Note: ttrss/include/rssfuncs.php lines 120-178 — PHP queries feed_url and deduplicates at the
64	          URL level before dispatching.  Python queries only ttrss_feeds.id and dispatches one task
65	          per feed row without URL-level deduplication — a structural redesign.
66	          Adapted: URL-level dedup omitted; ttrss_feeds.last_update_started guard prevents
67	          duplicate in-flight dispatches for the same feed row.
68	    """
69	    # Lazy Flask import — keeps celery_app.py independently importable (R18).
70	    # New: create_app() per task invocation — no PHP equivalent; required by Flask application
71	    #      factory pattern when running inside Celery workers (no shared app context).
72	    from ttrss import create_app
73	    from ttrss.extensions import db
74	
75	    app = create_app()
76	    with app.app_context():
77	        # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 120-131 — SQL for due feeds
78	        # update_interval=0 means "use user's DEFAULT_UPDATE_INTERVAL preference".
79	        # last_update_started guard prevents duplicate dispatch for in-flight feeds.
80	        from sqlalchemy import text
81	
82	        rows = db.session.execute(
83	            text(
84	                """
85	                SELECT DISTINCT ttrss_feeds.id
86	                FROM ttrss_feeds
87	                JOIN ttrss_users ON ttrss_feeds.owner_uid = ttrss_users.id
88	                JOIN ttrss_user_prefs
89	                  ON ttrss_users.id = ttrss_user_prefs.owner_uid
90	                 AND ttrss_user_prefs.pref_name = 'DEFAULT_UPDATE_INTERVAL'
91	                 AND ttrss_user_prefs.profile IS NULL
92	                WHERE

        # Source: rssfuncs.php lines 72-80 — login threshold: skip feeds for inactive users
        # Parameterised separately so DAEMON_UPDATE_LOGIN_LIMIT=0 disables the filter.
        login_thresh_sql = (
            "AND ttrss_users.last_login >= NOW() - CAST((:login_days || ' days') AS INTERVAL)"
            if DAEMON_UPDATE_LOGIN_LIMIT > 0
            else ""
        )

        rows = db.session.execute(
            text(
                f"""
                SELECT DISTINCT ttrss_feeds.id
                FROM ttrss_feeds
                JOIN ttrss_users ON ttrss_feeds.owner_uid = ttrss_users.id
                JOIN ttrss_user_prefs
                  ON ttrss_users.id = ttrss_user_prefs.owner_uid
                 AND ttrss_user_prefs.pref_name = 'DEFAULT_UPDATE_INTERVAL'
                 AND ttrss_user_prefs.profile IS NULL
                WHERE
                  (
                    (ttrss_feeds.update_interval = 0
                     AND ttrss_user_prefs.value != '-1'
                     AND (
                       ttrss_feeds.last_updated IS NULL
                       OR ttrss_feeds.last_updated
                          < NOW() - CAST((ttrss_user_prefs.value || ' minutes') AS INTERVAL)
                     ))
                    OR
                    (ttrss_feeds.update_interval > 0
                     AND (
                       ttrss_feeds.last_updated IS NULL
                       OR ttrss_feeds.last_updated
                          < NOW() - CAST((ttrss_feeds.update_interval || ' minutes') AS INTERVAL)
                     ))
                    OR ttrss_feeds.last_updated IS NULL
                  )
                  AND (
                    ttrss_feeds.last_update_started IS NULL
                    OR ttrss_feeds.last_update_started < NOW() - INTERVAL '10 minutes'
                  )
                  {login_thresh_sql}
                ORDER BY ttrss_feeds.last_updated NULLS FIRST
                LIMIT :limit
                """
            ),
            {"limit": DAEMON_FEED_LIMIT, "login_days": DAEMON_UPDATE_LOGIN_LIMIT},
        ).fetchall()
```

1272		function get_article_filters($filters, $title, $content, $link, $timestamp, $author, $tags) {
1273			$matches = array();
1274	
1275			foreach ($filters as $filter) {
1276				$match_any_rule = $filter["match_any_rule"];
1277				$inverse = $filter["inverse"];
1278				$filter_match = false;
1279	
1280				foreach ($filter["rules"] as $rule) {
1281					$match = false;
1282					$reg_exp = str_replace('/', '\/', $rule["reg_exp"]);
1283					$rule_inverse = $rule["inverse"];
1284	
1285					if (!$reg_exp)
1286						continue;
1287	
1288					switch ($rule["type"]) {
1289					case "title":
1290						$match = @preg_match("/$reg_exp/i", $title);
1291						break;
1292					case "content":
1293						// we don't need to deal with multiline regexps
1294						$content = preg_replace("/[\r\n\t]/", "", $content);
1295	
1296						$match = @preg_match("/$reg_exp/i", $content);
1297						break;
1298					case "both":
1299						// we don't need to deal with multiline regexps
1300						$content = preg_replace("/[\r\n\t]/", "", $content);
1301	
1302						$match = (@preg_match("/$reg_exp/i", $title) || @preg_match("/$reg_exp/i", $content));
1303						break;
1304					case "link":
1305						$match = @preg_match("/$reg_exp/i", $link);
1306						break;
1307					case "author":
1308						$match = @preg_match("/$reg_exp/i", $author);
1309						break;
1310					case "tag":
1311						foreach ($tags as $tag) {
1312							if (@preg_match("/$reg_exp/i", $tag)) {
1313								$match = true;
1314								break;
1315							}
1316						}
1317						break;
1318					}
1319	
1320					if ($rule_inverse) $match = !$match;
1321	
1322					if ($match_any_rule) {
1323						if ($match) {
1324							$filter_match = true;
1325							break;
1326						}
1327					} else {
1328						$filter_match = $match;
1329						if (!$match) {
1330							break;
1331						}
1332					}
1333				}
1334	
1335				if ($inverse) $filter_match = !$filter_match;
1336	
1337				if ($filter_match) {
1338					foreach ($filter["actions"] AS $action) {
1339						array_push($matches, $action);
1340	
1341						// if Stop action encountered, perform no further processing
1342						if ($action["type"] == "stop") return $matches;
1343					}
1344				}
1345			}
1346	
1347			return $matches;
1348		}
